data.title: Travelers dividend preview: set for another dividend increase
data.description:
Travelers Companies (TRV) is expected to announce a dividend hike at the end of June, extending its 20-year dividend growth streak based on historical trends.

Analysts expect a consensus annual dividend of $4.56 per share, implying a quarterly dividend of $1.14, which would represent a 3.64% increase from the prior payout of $1.10.

The company last paid a dividend of $1.10 per share in March 2026, representing an annual yield of 1.47%, and raised its dividend last June by 4.8% to $1.10 from $1.05.

The specialty materials company has delivered a 5-year dividend growth rate of approximately 5.29% and maintains a 4-year average payout ratio of 17.71%.

The property and casualty insurance company holds ratings of A for safety, A- for growth, D+ for yield, and A+ for dividend consistency.

The Travelers Companies (TRV) is scheduled to report its first-quarter 2026 results today, Thursday, April 16, 2026, before the market opens.

data.title: Citizens Financial Group GAAP EPS of $1.13 beats by $0.04, revenue of $2.17B beats by $10M
data.description:
• Citizens Financial Group press release (CFG): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.13 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $2.17B (+11.9% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Nonaccrual loans decreased 5% driven largely by a 12% decrease in commercial. The nonaccrual loans to total loansratio of 1.04% compares with 1.15% at March 31, 2025.• Net charge-offs of $138 million, or 39 basis points of average loans and leases compares with 58 basis points for firstquarter 2025. This reflects a decrease in retail, given charge-offs associated with a loan sale in first quarter 2025,and a decrease in commercial real estate.• Provision for credit losses of $140 million decreased compared with a $153 million provision in first quarter 2025reflecting the runoff of the Non-Core portfolio and improving credit trends and loan mix.• Allowance for credit losses of $2.2 billion decreased $27 million compared with March 31, 2025 given the benefit ofthe sale of Non-Core education loans, continued Non-Core runoff and other improvements in loan mix. Allowance forcredit losses ratio of 1.52% as of March 31, 2026 compares with 1.61% as of March 31, 2025.• The allowance for credit losses to nonaccrual loans and leases ratio of 146% compares with 140% as of March 31, 2025.

data.title: ERAYAK Power Solution to execute reverse stock split
data.description:
• ERAYAK Power Solution (RAYA) approved a reverse split of its Class A ordinary shares and Class B ordinary shares on a one-for-ten basis.
  • The company’s Class A Ordinary Shares will begin trading on the Nasdaq Stock Market LLC on a post-split basis on April 20, 2026, under the symbol “RAYA”.
  • The move is intended to increase the market price per share of the company’s Class A ordinary shares to allow the company to maintain its Nasdaq listing.
  • After giving effect to the reverse stock split, ERAYAK Power Solution (RAYA) expects to have ~978,474 Class A ordinary shares and 4,091 Class B ordinary shares issued and outstanding.
  • RAYA -23% premarket to $0.48
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AM Markets Need to Know: Private credit demand, Strait of Hormuz talks, and more
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Thursday as market sentiment improved on hopes for a potential peace deal between the U.S. and Iran.

Now, here are 5 news stories that broke overnight to watch out for:

Strait of Hormuz negotiations: Iran could consider allowing ships to sail freely through the Omani side of the Strait of Hormuz without risk of attack as part of proposals offered in negotiations with the United States, a source briefed by Tehran told Reuters. The source, who declined to be identified due to the sensitivity of the matter, said Iran could be willing to let ships use the other side of the narrow strait in Omani waters without hindrance, providing a deal is clinched to prevent renewed conflict.

U.S. oil export record: The Iran war is disrupting global oil markets and speeding up changes in energy trade, helping push the U.S. closer to becoming a net crude exporter for the first time since World War Two. U.S. crude exports are projected to reach a record high of 5.2M bbl/day in May, as Asian buyers snap up cargoes to offset the loss of Middle Eastern supply due to the Iran war, oil research firm Kpler said this week.

Iran war fiscal impact: White House Office of Management and Budget Director Russell Vought told lawmakers on Capitol Hill that he couldn’t estimate how much the Iran war would cost taxpayers. “We’re not ready to come to you with a request; we’re still working on it. We’re working through to figure out what’s needed in this fiscal year versus next fiscal year,” Vought told the House Budget Committee on Wednesday, according to CNBC.

Pentagon manufacturing talks: Senior Pentagon officials held talks with top executives of several companies, including General Motors (GM) and Ford (F), to discuss production of weapons and other military supplies, The Wall Street Journal reported. The officials also approached GE Aerospace (GE) and machinery maker Oshkosh (OSK), according to people familiar with the discussions.

Private credit demand: Private credit firms are likely to continue attracting capital despite recent redemption pressures due to the premium illiquid investments command, Goldman Sachs’ Kristin Olson said in a Bloomberg Television interview Thursday. The investment bank advises ultra-high-net-worth clients and family offices to allocate about 25% of a moderate-risk portfolio to alternative investments, an asset class that includes private credit, according to Olson, who is the global head of alternatives for wealth at Goldman.

data.title: Arthur J. Gallagher buys Bridge Insurance Brokers
data.description:
• Arthur J. Gallagher (AJG) has acquired UK-based commercial insurance broker, Bridge Insurance Brokers.
  • Terms of the transaction were not disclosed. Bridge specializes in real estate, construction, corporate and private client insurance, primarily for UK-based clients with a small international presence.

data.title: Pepsico Non-GAAP EPS of $1.61 beats by $0.06, revenue of $19.44B beats by $500M
data.description:
• Pepsico press release (PEP): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.61 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $19.44B (+8.5% Y/Y) beats by $500M.
  •  Net revenue increased 8.5% due to a 3.4-percentage-point benefit from foreignexchange translation, 2.6% organic revenue growth, and a 2.5-percentage-point netbenefit from acquisitions and divestitures. Organic revenue growth reflects the benefitsassociated with effective net pricing and a slight contribution from organic volumegrowth.
  • For 2026, the Company continues to expect:• Organic revenue to increase between 2 and 4 percent;• Core constant currency EPS to increase between 4 and 6 percent;• A core annual effective tax rate of approximately 22 percent;• Capital spending to be below 5 percent of net revenue;• A free cash flow conversion ratio of at least 80 percent; and• Total cash returns to shareholders of approximately $8.9 billion, comprised of dividends of $7.9billion and share repurchases of $1.0 billion
  • Shares +1.4% PM.

data.title: S&P 500 rebounds to record highs in near-record time after sharp pullback
data.description:
The S&P 500’s (SP500) rapid rebound to fresh record highs, just days after a 5%–10% drawdown, marks an exceptionally rare episode in market history, underscoring the resilience of risk appetite despite lingering macro uncertainty.

On Wednesday, the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ended at an all-time high as investors priced in good news on the U.S.-Iran conflict front while parsing more bank and financial earnings.

The speed of this recovery suggests that the recent decline was viewed less as a fundamental deterioration and more as a positioning-driven shakeout, with sidelined capital quickly redeployed once downside momentum faded.

The S&P 500 has surged more than 10% over the past 10 trading days, marking its best 10-day gain since the post-COVID lows of March 2020.

The move also reflects a market still conditioned to “buy the dip,” where liquidity, systematic flows, and strong earnings expectations combine to compress the duration of drawdowns. Short covering likely amplified the rebound, particularly as investors who had turned cautious were forced to chase the upside.

While the price action reinforces bullish momentum in the near term, the historical rarity of such swift recoveries suggests investors may need to remain alert to volatility re-emerging just as quickly.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was up 2.6% since the start of the year.

data.title: Taiwan Semi raises 2026 outlook after Q1 profit surges amid AI demand
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) raised its revenue outlook for 2026, driven by AI chip demand, after the global foundry's first quarter net income surged 58% year-over-year.

Shares of TSM climbed about 1% premarket on Thursday.

Q1 Metrics

TSMC — which produces chips for some of the world's largest tech companies, including Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD (AMD) — saw first quarter 2026 net revenue grow 40.6% year-over-year to $35.9B (NT$1.134T, +35.1% Y/Y).

“Our business in the first quarter was supported by strong demand for our leading-edge process technologies,” said Wendell Huang, senior vice president and CFO of TSM. 

The company's net income attributable to shareholders of the company soared 58.3% year-over-year to NT$572.48B, beating analysts estimates of NT$542.4B, according to a report from Bloomberg News.

"AI-related demand continues to be extremely robust," said TSM's CEO C.C. ​Wei on the earnings call.

TSM noted that its capital spending this year would be at ⁠the high end of earlier guidance of $52B to $56B.

Huang said on the call that TSM's first quarter capital expenditures totaled $11.1B.

"At TSMC, a higher level of capital expenditures is always correlated with higher growth opportunities in the following years. With our strong technology leadership and differentiation, we are well-positioned to capture the multi-year structural demand from the industry megatrends of 5G, AI, and HPC," said Huang on the call.

Middle East Impact

Huang said, given the recent situation in the Middle East, prices for certain chemicals and gasesare likely to increase. He noted that based on their current assessment, "there may be impact to our profitability, but it is too early to quantify the impact."

"We are also working closely with our suppliers to further strengthen the resiliency and sustainability of our supply chain. Thus, we do not expect any near-term impact on our operations for material supply. In terms of energy, TSMC works closely with Taipower and the Taiwan government to ensure a stable and sufficient energy supply," Huang added.

Outlook

For the second quarter, TSM expects revenue to be between $39B and $40.2B, whichrepresents a 10% sequential increase, or a 32% year-over-year increase at the midpoint. 

"Moving into second quarter 2026, we expect our business to be supported by continued strong demand for our leading-edge process technologies," said Huang in the press release.

For the full-year 2026, TSM now expects revenue to grow by above 30% in U.S. dollarterms, compared to revenue growth of nearly 30% it forecast in January. 

Huang noted that TSM remains committed to a sustainable and steadily increasing cash dividend per share on both an annual and quarterly basis.

SA Analyst Commentary

"From what I remember, the market was initially fearing that TSMC was nearing the peak in margins, but in Q1, we saw an expansion from ~58% in GP margin to over 66%, which is insanely solid for such a large entity. TSMC still has major pricing power, superior execution, and a business mix that is shifting toward higher-value products rather than commoditizing, which I don't think will vanish anywhere for the next few years, as we're witnessing the AI CAPEX stability across the board. I should note that TSM actually beat my earnings preview expectations, so I'd definitely rate the stock a "buy" right now after the print (as the stock price hasn't gone up too much; the valuation isn't stretched)," said Seeking Alpha analyst Oakoff Investments, Investing Group Leader for Beyond the Wall Investing.

Q1 Revenue by Technology

TSMC said 3nm process technology contributed 25% of total wafer revenue in the first-quarter, versus 22% in the year ago period, and 28% in the fourth quarter of 2025.

The 5nm process technology accounted for 36% of total wafer revenue, same as in the period a year ago, and 35% in the fourth quarter of 2025. Meanwhile, 7nm accounted for 13% of total wafer revenue, versus 15% a year earlier, and 14% in the fourth quarter of 2025.

Q1 Revenue by Geography

Revenue from China accounted for 7% of the total net revenue in the first quarter of 2026, same as in the year-ago period. It was 9% of the total net revenue in the fourth quarter of 2025.

North America accounted for 76% of total net revenue, compared to 77% a year earlier, and 74% in the fourth quarter of 2025.

Q1 Revenue by Platform

High Performance Computing, or HPC, represented 61% of net revenue, up from 59% in the first quarter of 2025. The company's smartphone segment represented 26% of net revenue in the fourth quarter of 2025, versus 28% in the year ago period.

data.title: Hims & Hers on track to extend gains as RFK Jr. seeks to loosen peptide regulations
data.description:
Hims & Hers (HIMS) shares rose as much as 11% in premarket trading on Thursday, on track to extend their ~14% rally on Wednesday, after HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said the FDA is seeking to remove 12 peptides from Category 2 restrictions.

“Today, we took long-overdue action to restore science, accountability, and the rule of law,” Kennedy said Wednesday in an X post. “This action begins to restore regulated access and will immediately begin shifting demand away from the black market.”

Kennedy Jr. said the U.S. Food and Drug Administration will take steps to allow certain peptides to be produced by compounding pharmacies, potentially reversing a Biden-era decision that cited safety concerns in restricting their broader manufacturing and sale.

The FDA said it will convene an advisory committee of independent experts in July to review evidence on certain peptides, according to a notice posted Wednesday. Such meetings typically precede regulatory decisions on drugs or medical products.

That could benefit companies like Hims & Hers, which have indicated plans to offer peptides if regulations are eased. Currently, aside from branded weight-loss drugs, most peptides are sold in the gray market. Hims acquired a peptide manufacturing facility in California in 2025 for an undisclosed amount.

Applauding the FDA's move towards regulatory clarity on peptide therapy, Hims chief medical officer Pat Carroll said: “The FDA’s plans to more clearly define the regulatory status of several peptides is an important step toward moving these treatments out of the gray market, and into more trusted channels overseen by vetted health-care professionals. We are actively exploring how to expand access in a way that will be aligned with FDA guidance and reflects our commitment to consumer safety, transparency, and clinical excellence.”

Potentially relevant tickers: Eli Lilly (LLY), Novo Nordisk (NVO), Hims & Hers Health (HIMS), Harrow (HROW), PeptiDream (PPTDF), PolyPeptide Group (PLYGF)

data.title: Euro Area inflation rises more than estimates in March
data.description:
• The inflation rate in the Euro Area increased to 2.60% in March from 1.9% in February, above estimates of 2.5%.
  • The Consumer Price Index increased 1.3% in March of 2026 over the previous month, above estimates of 1.2%.
  • In March 2026, services, energy, food, alcohol & tobacco and non-energy industrial goods contributed positively to the annual euro area inflation rate.
